name: Computer Networks 2
description: Protocols, architectures and layers of computer networks.

questions.question: In the DNS system, which server is the official last resort if the others cannot resolve a name?
options: The root name server.
The local DNS server.
The authoritative server.
The TLD-level server.

questions.question: Which HTTP method sends the user's input to the server by placing the data in the body of the request?
options: GET method.
PUT method.
POST method.
HEAD method.

questions.question: In the TCP protocol, what is the 'handshake' required before data transfer?
options: The prior setup of the connection between the client and server processes.
The immediate transmission of data using the destination IP address and port.
The lookup in the routing table to find the shortest physical path.
The abrupt termination of communication after sending the first data packet.

questions.question: What is a striking characteristic of the Peer-to-Peer (P2P) architecture for network applications?
options: The requirement of centralized routing.
The exclusive use of the UDP network protocol.
The absence of an always-on server.
The restriction of its reach to the local network (LAN).

questions.question: To correctly address a process running on a host, what is needed in addition to the IP address?
options: The port number associated with the process.
The MAC address of the sending host's card.
The encryption key for network access.
The domain name registered through DNS.

questions.question: HTTP is a 'stateless' protocol. How do web servers usually work around this to identify users and maintain sessions?
options: By keeping the TCP connection always open.
By switching from the TCP protocol to UDP.
By inserting cookies in the HTTP headers.
By requiring authentication at the network level.

questions.question: What is the main goal of using Web Cache servers in an institutional local network?
options: To encrypt all network connections.
To replace the network's local DNS service.
To guarantee immunity from cyberattacks.
To reduce the response time to the client.

questions.question: In file distribution via BitTorrent, what is the main function of the component called the 'tracker'?
options: To store the official, complete copy of the file.
To track and report which peers are in the torrent.
To limit a client's TCP connection rates.
To hide the client's IP through encryption.

questions.question: In network security, what characterizes a Denial of Service (DoS) attack?
options: The silent interception of packets on the network aiming to steal passwords.
The malicious injection of packets containing a fake source IP address.
The intentional overloading of a network resource to make it unavailable.
The alteration of digital signatures to compromise data integrity.

questions.question: Which protocol requires the sender to explicitly attach the destination IP and port to every packet sent, because it has no prior connection?
options: The TCP protocol (Transmission Control Protocol).
The UDP protocol (User Datagram Protocol).
The IP protocol (Internet Protocol v4 or v6).
The TLS protocol (Transport Layer Security).
